notes: Single batch (batch-1.json), no multi-part splits. Merge was a clean 1:1 carry-over: input 21 nodes/22 edges == output 21 nodes/22 edges. Merge script applied zero fixes and reported zero 'could not fix' items.
All 21 nodes have valid 'id', valid type 'schema', and valid complexity (simple/moderate/complex). No malformed, unknown-type, or unknown-complexity nodes to recover or remap.
11 'contains' edges (state-store->7 entity defs, hooks->4 hook-item defs) all reference existing nodes. Verified against the $defs structure of state-store.schema.json and hooks.schema.json. Sound.

11 'related' edges are all semantically grounded in actual cross-reference fields: install-config has profile+modules fields (->profiles/modules/components); profiles and components both have a modules field (->modules); install-state records profile/modules request + moduleId resolution (->install-config, ->modules); state-store embeds install-state; provenance attests skill origin (->skillVersion); plugin manifest declares skills/commands mapping to modules (->modules). No spurious edges.

Two 'related' edges are weaker but defensible, not wrong: package-manager->install-config (both install-time config consulted by the installer) and plugin->install-modules (manifest features map to installable units). Left as-is.

importMap is empty for all 10 files by design — these JSON Schema files use only internal '#/$defs/...' refs, zero inter-file imports. Merge script correctly recovered 0 'imports' edges. No cross-batch gaps to fill; added no speculative edges.

Observation only (not actioned, no $IMPORT_MAP backing): a conceptual provenance->state-store:skillRun link could arguably exist since both touch the skill telemetry loop, but adding it would be speculative — out of scope per the add-only-backed-edges rule.
No upstream data-quality concerns: 0% of nodes needed ID correction, complexity distribution is reasonable (not skewed to one value), no systemic LLM output anomalies.
